mnemonics.title: Iterator Has Next Remove
mnemonics.content: IHNR - Remember as 'I Have No Reason' to call next() without checking hasNext() first. Always check hasNext() before next() to avoid NoSuchElementException.

mnemonics.title: ListIterator Bidirectional Methods
mnemonics.content: ListIterator: FORWARD (hasNext, next) BACKWARD (hasPrevious, previous) ADD/SET (add, set) INDEX (nextIndex, previousIndex) - All in order of alphabet

acronyms.expansion: ConcurrentModificationException - thrown when collection modified during iteration
acronyms.usage: Iterator fails fast by detecting structural changes via modCount check

acronyms.expansion: First In First Out - Queue iterator order
acronyms.usage: Queue implementations return elements in insertion order

acronyms.expansion: Last In First Out - Stack iterator order
acronyms.usage: Stack/Deque can iterate from top to bottom

visualTips.visualization: Think of iterator as a book marker between elements: [A | B | C | D]. next() returns element to the right and moves marker. remove() removes element just returned.
ListIterator is like a cursor in text editor: |ABC means at start, A|BC after next(), AB|C after another next(). Can move left or right from current position.
Collection has internal counter (modCount). Iterator copies it. After each operation, iterator compares current modCount with stored copy - mismatch = concurrent modification detected.

analogies.analogy: Iterator is like a film projector cursor - it knows where you are in the film (collection), can show you next frame (next()), or rewind to beginning. Can't jump to middle without traversing.
It's like removing pages from a book while reading it - dangerous! Iterator.remove() is the safe way - like having the book hold your place while you carefully remove a page.
Fail-fast: alarm goes off if someone changes the playlist while you're listening. Fail-safe: you got a copy of the playlist, changes don't affect your iteration.
